purpose: Register specialist MCP capabilities that central governance may recommend or compose while leaving runtime implementation and credentials to independent services.

scope: Issue governance-safe status and receipt lookups.
logging_expectations: Mutation endpoints are forbidden in the central registry profile.
specialists.latency_cost_note: Low latency, low cost, suited for repeated policy lookups.

scope: Review interface hierarchy, clarity, and interaction patterns.
logging_expectations: Decision-worthy critiques must be persisted as findings or proposals.
specialists.latency_cost_note: Medium latency, useful when interaction exploration matters more than deterministic speed.

scope: Recommend motion and asset optimizations without flattening the design.
logging_expectations: Perf findings should include observable symptoms and mitigation notes.
specialists.latency_cost_note: Medium latency with potentially high savings on UX stability and infra waste.

scope: Review auth boundaries, secret exposure, and service-edge risk.
logging_expectations: Security findings require severity and owner route.
specialists.latency_cost_note: Higher review overhead, justified for production exposure and customer trust surfaces.

scope: Recover linked note context when a governed service explicitly references it.
logging_expectations: Every note-context use must name the originating link or note reference.
specialists.latency_cost_note: Low cost but high boundary sensitivity; use only when explicitly linked.
recommended_use_cases: Recovering operator intent from linked notes

hard_rules: Central governance may register and recommend MCP capabilities but must not host their runtime implementations here.
Each specialist recommendation must stay within declared data classes and auth expectations.
Specialist MCP selection is advisory until an independent service binds it into execution.
